name: Zerodha-Style Trading App — Multi-Environment Config
scenario: Refactor the scattered @Value configuration in a trading service to use @ConfigurationProperties records. Add @Validated constraints. Create dev/prod property files. Add @Profile beans for environment-specific services.
description: Replace all @Value fields in TradingService and NotificationService with two @ConfigurationProperties records. Add startup validation. Create dev (sandbox NSE, mock SMS), staging, and prod (live NSE, real SMS) configurations. Verify with @SpringBootTest.

core.description: `TradingConfig` @ConfigurationProperties(prefix='trading') @Validated with nested Market record (url, feedType, maxOrders) and nested RiskLimits record (maxOrderValueInRs, maxDailyLoss). @NotBlank on url, @Min/@Max on numeric fields.
`NotificationConfig` @ConfigurationProperties(prefix='notification') with nested Sms (url, apiKey, rateLimit) and Email (host, port, from). @Validated with constraints.

Three property files: application.properties (shared: app name, port, risk limits), application-dev.properties (sandbox NSE, mock credentials, H2, DEBUG), application-prod.properties (live NSE, env vars, PostgreSQL, WARN).
@Profile('dev') MockSmsService and @Profile('prod') LiveSmsService, both implementing SmsService interface. @Profile('dev') DevDataSeeder (CommandLineRunner seeding 3 test positions).
@SpringBootTest @ActiveProfiles('dev'): 4 assertions — market URL is sandbox, MockSmsService is injected, maxOrders is correct, H2 is the datasource.
@ConfigurationPropertiesScan on Application class. Inject both config records into TradingService via constructor.

bonus.description: Add a 'staging' profile: application-staging.properties with live NSE URL but mock credentials, PostgreSQL staging DB, INFO logging. @SpringBootTest @ActiveProfiles('staging') with 2 assertions.
Add `FeatureFlags` @ConfigurationProperties: enableAlgoTrading, enableOptionsChain, enableDarkMode. Dev: all true. Prod: only enableOptionsChain. Test both profiles.

Add @Scheduled @Profile('prod') EodReportJob (runs at 4PM weekdays) that reads TradingConfig.market().url(). Verify the job is NOT created in dev profile (@SpringBootTest @ActiveProfiles('dev') assertNull on the job bean).

data_to_use:
Config structure:
trading.market.url: https://sandbox.nseindia.com (dev), https://live.nseindia.com (prod)
trading.market.feed-type: MOCK (dev), WEBSOCKET (prod)
trading.market.max-orders: 10 (dev), 1000 (prod)
trading.risk-limits.max-order-value-in-rs: 100000
trading.risk-limits.max-daily-loss: 50000

notification.sms.url: https://mock-sms.dev (dev), https://api.textsms.in (prod)
notification.sms.api-key: dev_key (dev), ${SMS_LIVE_KEY} (prod)
notification.email.host: localhost (dev), smtp.gmail.com (prod)
example_output:
# Dev profile startup:
[main] Active profiles: dev
[DevDataSeeder] Seeding 3 test trading positions...
[main] TradingConfig: market.url=https://sandbox.nseindia.com
[main] NotificationConfig: sms.url=https://mock-sms.dev
[main] MockSmsService active (dev mode)

# Test output:
✓ market_url_isSandbox
✓ smsService_isMock
✓ maxOrders_isDevLimit (10)
✓ email_host_isLocalhost
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Nested @ConfigurationProperties record: add @Valid on nested record fields to propagate validation. @Profile bean: Spring only creates beans matching the active profile. @ActiveProfiles('dev') in test: exactly mirrors running with --spring.profiles.active=dev. @ConfigurationPropertiesScan: ONE annotation on Application discovers ALL @ConfigurationProperties in the package.
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. Show all property files. All @SpringBootTest tests must pass with @ActiveProfiles('dev').
tips_for_success: @ConfigurationPropertiesScan: put on Application class to auto-discover all @ConfigurationProperties
Nested record validation: put @Valid on the nested record field in the outer record
Dev property file: use realistic sandbox URLs — not placeholder strings
@Profile tests: @ActiveProfiles on the class applies to ALL test methods in that class
Constructor injection: TradingService(TradingConfig tc, NotificationConfig nc) — clean and testable
